title: VIDLab Research Group Meeting and Recognition Awards
summary: On Friday afternoon, April 24, VIDLab convened a full research group meeting and collegial gathering, highlighted by Dr. Akilan's remarks on time stewardship, resource planning, research priorities, and personal best achievements, followed by recognition awards for several students.
content:
Lab Celebration and Research Group Meeting

On Friday afternoon, April 24, 2026, VIDLab hosted a full-group research meeting and a warm, collegial gathering. The event brought together students and faculty for a meaningful exchange of ideas, reflection, and recognition.

In his address, Dr. Akilan reflected on time stewardship, resource planning, and the central priorities of effective research practice, emphasizing the importance of pursuing personal best achievements with discipline and purpose.

He then presented appreciation awards to several students in recognition of their recent milestones and contributions, including the achievements of students.

title: Celebrating Achievements and Conference Acceptances
summary: VIDLab recognizes significant milestones by Christopher Silver, Gaeul Han, Ebenezer Penoo, Dr. Kanchan Keisham, and Thenukan Pathmanathan, alongside acceptances to Canadian AI 2026 and CRV 2026.
content:
Accomplishments and Publications

VIDLab is pleased to recognize several outstanding milestones achieved by our team members:

  • Christopher Silver for the successful completion of the Lab2Market Validate program (Winter 2026).
  • Gaeul Han for a successful MSc defense and acceptance of publications at the 39th Canadian Conference on Artificial Intelligence (Canadian AI 2026) and the 3rd Asia Symposium on Image and Graphics (ASIG 2025).
  • Ebenezer Penoo for successful MSc completion and acceptance of a publication at the 39th Canadian Conference on Artificial Intelligence (Canadian AI 2026).
  • Dr. Kanchan Keisham for acceptance of a publication at the 39th Canadian Conference on Artificial Intelligence (Canadian AI 2026).
  • Thenukan Pathmanathan for acceptance of a publication at the 23rd Conference on Robots and Vision (CRV 2026).

These accomplishments reflect sustained effort and scholarly rigor, and we are proud to celebrate them as a lab community.

Conference Program Outlines

The 39th Canadian Conference on Artificial Intelligence (Canadian AI 2026) program features keynote addresses, tutorials, technical paper sessions, workshops, and community events that bring together AI researchers and practitioners from across Canada and beyond.

The 23rd Conference on Robots and Vision (CRV 2026) program includes invited speakers, oral and poster sessions, demonstrations, and networking opportunities focused on robotics and computer vision research.

Canadian AI 2026 Program CRV 2026 Program

title: ICDLT 2026 - Call for Papers and Participation
summary: Join us in celebrating the International Conference on Deep Learning Technologies (ICDLT) 2026! Held July 17–19 in Kunming, China, this flagship conference brings together researchers and innovators. Submit your papers and register now.
content:
International Conference on Deep Learning Technologies 2026

We are excited to share information about the International Conference on Deep Learning Technologies (ICDLT) 2026, the 10th edition of this premier conference.

Conference Details:
Date: July 17–19, 2026
Location: Kunming, China

ICDLT brings together researchers, scholars, and scientists from around the world for face-to-face exchange on the latest deep learning technologies and applications.

Technical Tracks Include:

  • Deep learning models and algorithms
  • Machine learning theory and technology
  • Deep/machine learning applications
  • Responsible AI, security, and governance

This conference creates a strong platform for academic and industry collaboration, offering an excellent opportunity for researchers and students to present their work and network with peers.

Important Information: The conference features structured submission and review timelines, publication in conference proceedings, and open calls through the official CFP and submission portals.

View Call for Papers | Submission Information

Visit ICDLT 2026

Image courtesy of ICDLT 2026 Conference

title: Welcome and Congratulations to Kenneth Shahi & Kamran Yaghoubian
summary: Join us in congratulating Kenneth Shahi and Kamran Yaghoubian on receiving the prestigious NSERC Undergraduate Student Research Award (USRA).
content:
NSERC USRA Awardees

We are delighted to congratulate Kenneth Shahi and Kamran Yaghoubian for being awarded the highly competitive and prestigious NSERC Undergraduate Student Research Award (USRA). This achievement highlights their academic excellence and strong research potential.

The NSERC USRA is designed to nurture the research careers of bright, promising undergraduate students by providing them with research work experience in an academic setting. Their dedication and hard work have truly paid off.

Congratulations, Kenneth and Kamran! We look forward to your continued success and ongoing research contributions at VIDLab. Thank you to everyone who supported them throughout their academic journey!

View Our Interns

content:

On December 4, 2025, VIDLab hosted a luncheon with guest speaker Christopher Silver, who shared recent insights from his research and experiences. The event brought together faculty, students, and collaborators for an informal discussion and networking lunch.

title: Chee Mei Ling (Irene)'s Paper Accepted at ASIG 2025
summary: Congratulations to Chee Mei Ling (Irene), Thangarajah Akilan, and Aparna Ravinda Phalke for the acceptance of their paper to ASIG 2025.
content:
Paper Acceptance at ASIG 2025

Congratulations to Chee Mei Ling (Irene), Thangarajah Akilan, and Aparna Ravinda Phalke for the acceptance of their paper titled 'Dual Atrous Separable Convolution for Improving Agricultural Semantic Segmentation' to the 3rd Asia Symposium on Image and Graphics (ASIG 2025), to be held during November 28-30, 2025 in Osaka, Japan.

Learn More About ASIG 2025

title: Gaeul Han's Paper Accepted at ASIG 2025
summary: Congratulations to Gaeul Han, Thangarajah Akilan, and Arunya Prasantha Senadeera for the acceptance of their paper to ASIG 2025.
content:
Paper Acceptance at ASIG 2025

Congratulations to Gaeul Han, Thangarajah Akilan, and Arunya Prasantha Senadeera for the acceptance of their paper titled 'SEA-YOLO: Thermal Imaging Object Detection via Spatial Edge Attention for Low-visibility Autonomous Driving' to the 3rd Asia Symposium on Image and Graphics (ASIG 2025), to be held during November 28-30, 2025 in Osaka, Japan.

Learn More About ASIG 2025

title: Congratulations to Our 2025 Graduates at Lakehead University Convocation
summary: VIDLab celebrates the academic achievements of Christopher Silver, Nusrat Jahan, Tharrengini Suresh, and Harish Sundaralingam who attended the Spring 2025 Convocation ceremony at Lakehead University.
content:
Lakehead University Spring 2025 Convocation Ceremony - Thunder Bay Campus

Celebrating Academic Excellence and Research Achievements!

We are incredibly proud to congratulate our outstanding graduates Christopher Silver, Nusrat Jahan, Tharrengini Suresh, and Harish Sundaralingam who participated in Lakehead University's Spring 2025 Convocation ceremonies. Their dedication to research and academic excellence exemplifies the high standards of VIDLab.

The Spring 2025 Convocation ceremonies at Lakehead University's Thunder Bay campus took place from May 29-30, 2025, celebrating the achievements of graduating students across all faculties. Our graduates participated in multiple ceremony sessions, with each ceremony running approximately 2.5 hours and followed by receptions at the campus surrounding Lake Tamblyn and inside the Agora (University Centre).

Dr. Akilan commented: 'Watching our students achieve their academic milestones is one of the most rewarding aspects of leading VIDLab. Christopher, Nusrat, Tharrengini, and Harish have demonstrated exceptional research capabilities and dedication. Their work in areas ranging from semantic segmentation to object detection will have lasting impact on the field.'

Graduate Achievements:

  • Christopher Silver: Completed his MSc in Electrical and Computer Engineering with research focus on advanced computer vision algorithms and deep learning applications
  • Nusrat Jahan: Completed her MSc in Electrical and Computer Engineering with research focus on 'An Improved Semi-Supervised Learning Framework for Image Semantic Segmentation'
  • Tharrengini Suresh: Graduated with distinction with her MSc in Electrical and Computer Engineering, contributing to 'Semantic Segmentation with Edge-Context Enrichment and Attention Strategies: A Refined Framework for Improved Performance in Complex Visual Environments'
  • Harish Sundaralingam: Achieved his MSc in Electrical and Computer Engineering, specializing in 'Enhancing Object Detection in Complex Scenes: A Semantic Segmentation-aware Framework with Attention-Gating for Improved Small Object Detection'
Left: Nusrat Jahan, Right: Christopher Silver
Tharrengini Suresh
Harish Sundaralingam

The Thunder Bay convocation ceremonies were held at Lakehead University's campus (955 Oliver Road), with graduates from the Faculty of Natural Resources Management, Faculty of Science and Environmental Studies, and Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities participating in the May 30th morning ceremony. Each ceremony provided an exceptional opportunity for graduates to join their classmates and professors in celebrating their accomplishments at Lakehead.

We wish our graduates continued success in their future endeavors and look forward to their continued contributions to the field of intelligent systems and data analytics.

Learn More About Lakehead Convocation

title: Dr. Akilan Selected as Invited Speaker at ICSIP 2025
summary: Dr. Akilan, Director of VIDLab, has been selected as an invited speaker at the International Conference on Signal and Image Processing (ICSIP) 2025.
content:
Invited Speaker at ICSIP 2025

We are delighted to announce that Dr. Akilan, Director of the Vision Intelligence and Data Lab (VIDLab), has been selected as an invited speaker at the International Conference on Signal and Image Processing (ICSIP) 2025 on the topic 'The Emergence of Self-supervised Learning in Medical Image Semantic Segmentation'.

For more information - Welcome to 10th ICSIP 2025

Learn More About ICSIP 2025

title: Christopher Silver's Research Published in Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence Journal
summary: Congratulations to Christopher Silver for the acceptance of his work on thermal imaging-based fall detection in a prestigious international journal.
content:
Research Publication Success!

Congratulations to Christopher Silver for the acceptance of the work titled 'Thermal Fall 66: A Robust Dataset for Thermal Imaging-based Fall Detection and Eldercare' in the Engineering Applications of Artificial Intelligence Journal.

This research contributes significantly to the field of eldercare technology and thermal imaging applications, demonstrating VIDLab's continued excellence in developing practical AI solutions for healthcare and safety applications.

View Paper
